data.title: As a developer or agent, ticket deletion behavior is documented in ticket-cli-reference.md and CLAUDE.md reflects the deleted terminal status
data.description:
Story: Update documentation so the ticket delete subcommand and its behavior are discoverable, and CLAUDE.md reflects deleted as a terminal status alongside closed.

Done Definitions:
- ticket-cli-reference.md has a 'ticket delete' entry under the CLI subcommands section documenting: syntax (ticket delete <id> [--user-approved]), behavior (marks deleted + archived, removes blocker deps), restrictions (non-deleted children block deletion), and the human-in-the-loop gate (--user-approved required; absent = instructional error)
- ticket-cli-reference.md notes that --user-approved is intentionally undocumented in --help output and is discovered only by running the command without it
- CLAUDE.md Quick Reference table includes 'ticket delete' row
- CLAUDE.md ticket status terminology notes 'deleted' as a terminal status alongside 'closed'
- No implementation details about the --user-approved flag are included in CLAUDE.md (it is intentionally undocumented there too -- only ticket-cli-reference.md documents the discovery mechanism)
- Review: documentation changes reviewed via /dso:review before close

Considerations:
- [Dependency] Requires S1, S2, S3 to be complete so the documented behavior matches the implemented behavior
- [Scope] This story is documentation-only -- no code changes
